data.content:
# Implementation Plan: Add acceptance criteria as first-class knot field

## Overview
Add an `acceptance` field (`Option<String>`) to the knot data model, paralleling the existing `description`/`body` pattern. This involves changes across 8 layers: schema, structs, events, CLI, app logic, rehydration, sync, UI/prompt rendering.

## Steps

### 1. Schema migration (src/db.rs)
- Add v12 migration: `ALTER TABLE knot_hot ADD COLUMN acceptance TEXT;`
- Increment `MIGRATIONS` array

### 2. Data structs (src/db.rs)
- Add `pub acceptance: Option<String>` to `KnotCacheRecord` (after `description`)
- Add `pub acceptance: Option<&'a str>` to `UpsertKnotHot` (after `description`)
- Update `upsert_knot_hot()` SQL INSERT/UPDATE to include `acceptance` column
- Update `get_knot_hot()` and any SELECT queries to read `acceptance`

### 3. KnotView (src/app.rs)
- Add `pub acceptance: Option<String>` to `KnotView` (after `description`)
- Update all places that construct `KnotView` from `KnotCacheRecord` to map the field

### 4. Event kind (src/events/mod.rs)
- Add `KnotAcceptanceSet` variant to `FullEventKind`
- Map to `"knot.acceptance_set"` in `as_str()`

### 5. Create knot (src/app.rs)
- Add `acceptance` parameter to `create_knot` / `create_knot_with_options`
- If acceptance is provided, emit a `KnotAcceptanceSet` event after creation
- Or include acceptance in the `knot.created` event data (follow description pattern)

### 6. Update knot (src/app.rs)
- Add `pub acceptance: Option<String>` to `UpdateKnotPatch`
- In `update_knot()`, handle acceptance like description: compare with current, emit `KnotAcceptanceSet` event if changed
- Wire through `update_knot_metadata()` or equivalent

### 7. CLI flags (src/cli.rs)
- Add `--acceptance` flag to `NewArgs`: `#[arg(long, help = "Acceptance criteria text.")]`
- Add `--acceptance` flag to `UpdateArgs`: `#[arg(long, help = "Set acceptance criteria.")]`

### 8. Write dispatch (src/write_dispatch.rs)
- Add `acceptance` field to `NewOperation` and `UpdateOperation` (src/write_queue.rs)
- Wire `args.acceptance.clone()` in `operation_from_command()`

### 9. Write queue execution (src/write_dispatch.rs or wherever operations are executed)
- Pass `acceptance` from `NewOperation` through to `create_knot`
- Pass `acceptance` from `UpdateOperation` through to `UpdateKnotPatch`

### 10. Rehydration (src/app.rs)
- In `apply_rehydrate_event()`, add case for `"knot.acceptance_set"`:
  ```
  projection.acceptance = optional_string(data.get("acceptance"));
  projection.updated_at = event.occurred_at.clone();
  ```
- Add `acceptance: Option<String>` to `RehydrateProjection`

### 11. Sync (src/sync/apply.rs)
- Add case for `"knot.acceptance_set"` in sync event handling:
  ```
  self.apply_metadata_update(&event.knot_id, |record| {
      record.acceptance = optional_string(data.get("acceptance"));
  })?;
  ```
- Add `acceptance: Option<String>` to `MetadataProjection`

### 12. UI show (src/ui.rs)
- In `knot_show_fields()`, add acceptance display after description:
  ```
  if let Some(acceptance) = knot.acceptance.as_deref() {
      fields.push(ShowField::new("acceptance", acceptance));
  }
  ```

### 13. Prompt rendering (src/prompt.rs)
- In `render_prompt_inner()`, after description section, render acceptance:
  ```
  if let Some(acceptance) = knot.acceptance.as_deref().filter(|a| !a.is_empty()) {
      out.push_str("## Acceptance Criteria\n\n");
      out.push_str(acceptance);
      out.push_str("\n\n");
  }
  ```
- This is distinct from profile-level acceptance (from workflow prompts). Knot-level acceptance appears in the knot prompt header; profile-level acceptance appears in the skill body.

### 14. Tests
- Unit test: create knot with acceptance, verify round-trip via show
- Unit test: update knot acceptance, verify event emitted and field updated
- Unit test: rehydration applies acceptance_set event correctly
- Unit test: sync applies acceptance_set event correctly
- Unit test: prompt rendering includes acceptance criteria section
- Unit test: UI show displays acceptance field
- Coverage target: maintain ≥95%

## Complexity
- Medium. Follows well-established patterns (description, priority, etc.)
- ~12 files touched, but each change is mechanical and pattern-following
- No architectural decisions needed — this is a straight field addition

## Risks
- Schema migration ordering: must be v12 (after v11 workflow_id). Low risk.
- Profile-level vs knot-level acceptance: could be confusing in prompt output. Mitigate by rendering them in distinct sections with clear labels.
- Backward compatibility: old databases will get NULL acceptance via ALTER TABLE default. Safe.

## Files to modify
1. `src/db.rs` - schema migration, structs, queries
2. `src/app.rs` - KnotView, create/update logic, rehydration
3. `src/events/mod.rs` - new event kind
4. `src/cli.rs` - CLI flags
5. `src/write_queue.rs` - operation structs
6. `src/write_dispatch.rs` - CLI-to-operation wiring
7. `src/sync/apply.rs` - sync event handling
8. `src/ui.rs` - show display
9. `src/prompt.rs` - prompt rendering
10. `src/app/tests.rs` - unit tests
11. `src/app/tests_error_paths.rs` - error path tests (if relevant)
12. `src/ui_tests_ext.rs` - UI tests
